current: On X=[0,1] with P_X=Lebesgue, fix h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)}, q_n=(1/4 if beta_ag=0 else h_n^{beta_ag}), and an active block B_n=[0, c_B h_n^alpha] with P_X(B_n)=c_B h_n^alpha. The two laws P_{n,sigma}, sigma in {+,-}, share P_X, share the logging propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n and e(x)=1/2 off B_n, and share an off-block outcome law with common optimal label 1 and contrast tau_0 > u_0. On B_n set mu_0(x)=0 and let the treated outcome on the active treated cell {X in B_n, A=1} satisfy P(Y=1 | X in B_n, A=1)=(1+sigma h_n)/2, so tau_{P_{n,sigma}}=sigma h_n on B_n. The laws agree everywhere off the treated active cell. Constants are chosen with 8 C_B C_Q < log 5.
proposed: On X=[0,1] with P_X=Lebesgue, fix h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)} and q_n=q_0 in (0,1/2] when beta_ag=0, while q_n=C_Q h_n^{beta_ag} capped by 1/2 when beta_ag>0. Let B_n=[0,c_B h_n^alpha]. The two laws P_{n,sigma}, sigma in {+,-}, share P_X and the propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n and e(x)=1/2 off B_n. Choose tau_0 in (u_0,2). Off B_n set, for example, mu_1=tau_0/2 and mu_0=-tau_0/2, so the common optimal label is 1. On B_n set mu_0=0 and let Y(1) be Rademacher on {-1,1} with P(Y(1)=1|X in B_n)=(1+sigma h_n)/2, so mu_1=sigma h_n and tau=sigma h_n; let Y(0) have mean 0 and support [-1,1]. Draw A independently of the potential outcomes given X and set Y=AY(1)+(1-A)Y(0). Choose c_B and C_Q small enough for the margin, overlap, and divergence constants.
reason: The active treated outcome must have mean sigma h_n for the stated contrast. Reading the old Bernoulli sentence as a {0,1}-valued outcome would give mean (1+sigma h_n)/2 rather than sigma h_n. The correction makes the Rademacher support explicit and also fixes the beta_ag=0 case, where h_n^0 should be a fixed admissible propensity rather than 1.

current: Let kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c satisfying r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define zeta(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{1/2-lambda/2,c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma)} with the closed-form optimizer in lem:clip-balance-exponent. Define r_plug=kappa zeta(a,c,gamma), q_n=n^{-lambda_*} capped at 1/4, and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),r_plug}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, set q_n to a fixed value below the floor and r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
proposed: Let kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and polynomial nuisance exponents a,c with r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, set zeta(a,c,gamma)=min{1/2,(1+2 gamma a)/(2(1+gamma)),(c+2 gamma a)/(1+2 gamma)} and lambda_*=max{0,2 gamma (zeta-a)}. Use the clip q_n=min{1/4,n^{-lambda_*}}. Define r_plug=kappa zeta(a,c,gamma) and r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),r_plug}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, choose any fixed q<underline_p and set r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
reason: The open-ended feasible-upper node asks for the derived construction. The correction records the optimized closed form rather than leaving zeta as an unevaluated supremum and makes the nuisance-exponent dependence explicit.
